title: The Sand Tender: The Hammerhead's Tale

"'''The Sand Tender: The Hammerhead's Tale'''" is a short story written by [[Dave Wolverton]] from ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', which was published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[August]] [[1995]]. The story is about [[Momaw Nadon/Legends|Momaw Nadon]] and takes place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

==Plot summary==
[[Momaw Nadon/Legends|Momaw Nadon]], an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]], resides on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] after being exiled from his home [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], for revealing Ithorian [[Garden/Legends|gardening]] secrets to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Alima]], albeit under threat of [[Torture/Legends|torture]]. While living on Tatooine, Nadon still honored the Ithorian traditions and tried to adapt large [[Tree/Legends|trees]] from Ithor to the hot dry weather on Tatooine.

On Tatooine, just after the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tatooine]], he met Alima, who was searching for the missing [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Alima threatened to [[Death/Legends|kill]] his beloved trees if he did not help him find those two droids. The pacifistic Nadon attempted to kill Alima, but because of Nadon's lacking experience with [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]], the attempt failed and Nadon was humiliated by Alima once more. Finally, Nadon discovered the truth when he saw old [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Ben Kenobi]] in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]]. The old [[Gender/Legends|man]] had the wanted droids. Nadon then hid in the background when [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] tried to stop [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] from escaping with Kenobi, the droids and a [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|young farm boy]].

After losing the droids, the [[Imperial Prefect]] searched for a scapegoat for this failure. Nadon then appeared on the scene and told the Imperial Prefect that he told Alima about ''Millennium Falcon'' and Alima didn't report that information. Nadon was willing to swear under oath and ready to testify that in [[Imperial Court/Legends|Imperial court]]. The officer, taking justice in his own hand, shot Alima, killing him. Nadon was horrified and saddened at what he had done, but a small part of him was glad, for he and his trees were safe. To make up for this, he took two cell samples from Alima's corpse for later [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]]. In keeping with the [[Law of Life]], Nadon made two clones, so that Alima would pay his debt to the Ithorians—and Nadon would pay his to Alima.
